title: Psilocybin triggers an activity-dependent rewiring of large-scale cortical networks.

assets.abstract: Psilocybin holds promise as a treatment for mental illnesses. One dose of psilocybin induces structural remodeling of dendritic spines in the medial frontal cortex in mice. The dendritic spines would be innervated by presynaptic neurons, but the sources of these inputs have not been identified. Here, using monosynaptic rabies tracing, we map the brain-wide distribution of inputs to frontal cortical pyramidal neurons. We discover that psilocybin's effect on connectivity is network specific, stren

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Psychedelics — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states, predictive processing decoupling.
